headline: Wind down for deeper sleep
subtext: A short routine to tell the nervous system the day is done.
steps: Dim the lights an hour before bed to cue your brain.
Park tomorrow's worries on paper, out of your head.
Slow the breath, lengthening each exhale, until sleep comes.
caption:
Since poor sleep amplifies pain, a calm wind-down is one of the highest-value habits in recovery.

Try a simple routine. An hour before bed, dim the lights to cue melatonin. Write down tomorrow's worries so your mind can let them go. Then slow your breathing, making each exhale a little longer than the inhale, which nudges the nervous system out of alert and towards rest.

For chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, protecting sleep protects everything else.

More practices at https://neurowaze.health

#ChronicPain #Neuroplasticity #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Wind-down routines that reduce evening arousal support deeper sleep, which in turn lowers next-day pain sensitivity. Find guided practices in the Neurowaze Resources area: https://neurowaze.health
